title: Base and degenerate cases: c=1, c=2, rank r=1, empty B_c

comments.author: human
comments.body: For faithfulness and completeness, handle: (i) $c=1$: $B_1=X$, $N_1=\langle X\rangle^F=F=\gamma_1$ (immediate). (ii) $c=2$: $B_2=\{[x_j,x_i]:j>i\}$ which normally generate $[F,F]=\gamma_2$ directly (no lifting needed) — note this case is special. (iii) Rank $r=1$: $F=\mathbb Z$, $\gamma_c=1$ for $c\ge2$ and $B_c=\varnothing$ so $N_c=1$ — the empty-set / vacuous case must be addressed. (iv) General check that $B_c\ne\varnothing$ exactly when $\gamma_c\ne\gamma_{c+1}$ (Witt formula). These cases are easy but must be stated, not skipped.
